title: ds-viz-map - option matrix (dark)
description: Test bench for splunk.map. Verifies all top-level options (backgroundColor, center, zoom, scaleUnit, showScale, showBaseLayer, showZoomControls, baseLayerTileServer, baseLayerTileServerType) and per-layer options (type=marker|bubble|choropleth, latitude, longitude, bubbleSize, areaIds, areaValues, source, dataColors, dataValues, seriesColors, additionalTooltipFields, tooltipHeaderField, resultLimit, choroplethOpacity, choroplethStrokeColor, choroplethEmptyAreaColor). Test data is synthesized inline via makeresults so no external lookups are required for marker/bubble panels.

ds_marker_synth.name: synthesized lat lon value rows for marker panels

ds_bubble_synth.name: synthesized lat lon for bubble panel - geostats produces latitude longitude geobin columns the bubble layer expects

ds_bubble_get_only.name: synthesized GET-only traffic for bubble Data Colors panel - mirrors the Splunk doc example

ds_choropleth_synth.name: synthesized country counts uses geom command - bundled with Splunk core - lookup matches on country full name not ISO-2

options.query: | makeresults count=8 | streamstats count as i | eval country=case(i==1,"United States",i==2,"United Kingdom",i==3,"France",i==4,"Germany",i==5,"Japan",i==6,"Brazil",i==7,"Australia",i==8,"Norway"), count=case(i==1,520,i==2,310,i==3,180,i==4,420,i==5,140,i==6,260,i==7,90,i==8,75) | geom geo_countries featureIdField=country | fields country count geom

ds_choropleth_geom_string.name: synthesized country counts geom column serialized as string - lookup matches on country full name not ISO-2

options.query: | makeresults count=6 | streamstats count as i | eval country=case(i==1,"United States",i==2,"United Kingdom",i==3,"France",i==4,"Germany",i==5,"Brazil",i==6,"Norway"), count=case(i==1,520,i==2,310,i==3,180,i==4,420,i==5,260,i==6,75) | geom geo_countries featureIdField=country | where isnotnull(geom) | eval geom=tostring(geom) | fields country count geom

ds_choropleth_world_full.name: synthesized world choropleth uses source=geo url no geom needed

options.query: | makeresults count=10 | streamstats count as i | eval country=case(i==1,"US",i==2,"GB",i==3,"FR",i==4,"DE",i==5,"JP",i==6,"BR",i==7,"AU",i==8,"NO",i==9,"CA",i==10,"IN"), count=case(i==1,820,i==2,460,i==3,380,i==4,540,i==5,290,i==6,310,i==7,180,i==8,160,i==9,420,i==10,650) | table country count

ds_choropleth_us_states.name: synthesized US state counts uses geo us source

options.query: | makeresults count=10 | streamstats count as i | eval state=case(i==1,"CA",i==2,"TX",i==3,"NY",i==4,"FL",i==5,"WA",i==6,"IL",i==7,"PA",i==8,"OH",i==9,"GA",i==10,"VA"), count=case(i==1,920,i==2,780,i==3,650,i==4,540,i==5,420,i==6,360,i==7,310,i==8,280,i==9,240,i==10,200) | table state count

options.markdown:
# `splunk.map` - option matrix

The geographic map visualization. Renders interactive Leaflet-style maps with one or more **layers** (`marker`, `bubble`, `choropleth`) on top of a base tile server.

**Top-level options (10.4 PDF + Splunk Cloud Maps doc):**

- `backgroundColor`, `center` (`[lat, lon]`), `zoom`, `scaleUnit` (`metric`|`imperial`), `showScale`
- `showBaseLayer`, `showZoomControls`, `showCoordinates`, `icon`
- `baseLayerTileServer` (URL), `baseLayerTileServerType` (`vector`|`raster`)
- `layers` (array of layer objects)

**Per-layer options:**

- `type` (`marker`|`bubble`|`choropleth`)
- `latitude`, `longitude`, `bubbleSize`, `areaIds`, `areaValues`, `source`
- `dataColors`, `dataValues`, `seriesColors`
- `additionalTooltipFields`, `tooltipHeaderField`, `resultLimit`
- Choropleth-only: `choroplethOpacity`, `choroplethStrokeColor`, `choroplethEmptyAreaColor`

**Layer-vs-SPL pairing rules (Splunk doc, learned the hard way):**

- `marker` → SPL must use `table` (or any source with `lat`/`lon` columns); set `latitude` and `longitude` explicitly on the layer.
- `bubble` → SPL must use `geostats latfield=... longfield=... <metric> by <dim>`. The bubble layer reads `latitude`/`longitude`/`geobin` columns implicitly - **explicit `latitude`/`longitude` bindings are silently ignored** and the panel renders empty.
- `choropleth` → SPL must end in `geom geo_countries featureIdField=...` OR set `source: "geo://default/world"|"geo://default/us"` on the layer (not both). Always include `tooltipHeaderField` to wire up hover surfaces.

**bubbleSize gotcha:** the docs default `frameBySeriesTypes("number")` is the working binding shape - `seriesByName("count")` returns a single series and the layer renders points without sized bubbles. Use `frameWithoutSeriesNames('geobin','latitude','longitude') | frameBySeriesTypes('number')` against a `geostats` source instead.

**Multi-layer caveat:** stacking `marker` + `bubble` in the same `layers[]` is unreliable in current Dashboard Studio builds - the second layer commonly silently disappears. The official Splunk doc examples never stack them either. Use **one layer type per panel** and compose richer maps by placing multiple panels in the dashboard layout.

viz_marker_default.title: 1. Marker layer - default (no center/zoom)
viz_marker_default.description: Bare marker layer. center/zoom auto-fit from data. lat/lon bound by name.

viz_marker_centered.title: 2. Marker layer - explicit center + zoom
viz_marker_centered.description: center=[20, 0] (mid-Atlantic), zoom=1.5. Explicit framing for repeatable views.

viz_marker_colored.title: 3. Marker layer - dataColors via rangeValue(config)
viz_marker_colored.description: Marker fill driven by bytes value -> green / yellow / red. Demonstrates the canonical 'thresholded marker' recipe from the PDF.

viz_bubble.title: 4. Bubble layer - sized by `count` (geostats pattern)
viz_bubble.description: Bubble layers must be paired with the `geostats` SPL command - the layer expects the columns it produces (`latitude`, `longitude`, `geobin`) and picks them up implicitly. **Do NOT** set `latitude`/`longitude` on the layer for `type: bubble` - those bindings are silently ignored and the map renders empty. SPL pattern: `... | geostats latfield=lat longfield=lon count by method`. `bubbleSize` uses `frameWithoutSeriesNames('latitude','longitude','geobin')` to expose only the metric columns - `frameBySeriesTypes('number')` then encodes them as bubble size.

viz_choropleth.title: 5. Choropleth layer - country shading
viz_choropleth.description: Choropleth via the bundled `geom geo_countries` lookup. SPL must end in `geom geo_countries featureIdField=country` and the result must include the `geom` column. **Critical**: `geo_countries` keys on the full English country name (`United States`, `United Kingdom`, `Brazil`), NOT on ISO-2 codes (`US`, `GB`, `BR`) - ISO-2 keys produce all-NULL `geom` and the panel renders empty. areaIds + areaValues bind the country name and the metric. `tooltipHeaderField` ensures the polygon hover surface is wired up - without it, some Dashboard Studio versions render the polygons but provide no tooltip target and the panel can appear empty.

viz_imperial.title: 6. scaleUnit=imperial, showScale=true
viz_imperial.description: Bottom-left scale bar shows miles instead of metres.

viz_no_scale.title: 7. showScale=false
viz_no_scale.description: Hides the scale bar entirely - used for compact KPI maps where the scale would clutter the panel.

viz_result_limit.title: 8. resultLimit=5
viz_result_limit.description: Per-layer cap on rendered points. With 12 cities in the data source, only 5 markers should appear.

viz_bubble_data_colors.title: 9. Bubble Type Map - Data Colors (canonical splunk-ui example)
viz_bubble_data_colors.description: Verbatim adaptation of the **Bubble Map - dynamic coloring** sample from the Splunk Dashboard Studio Maps doc. Bubbles are sized implicitly by the `count` column from `geostats`, then **coloured** via `dataColors: "> dataValues | rangeValue(dataColorsEditorConfig)"`. The `dataValues` token is a frame the bubble layer auto-exposes - paired with `rangeValue(<context-key>)` it gives traffic-light bucketing without extra `seriesByName(...)` plumbing. NB: marker + bubble in the same `layers[]` is unreliable in current builds (the second layer often disappears); use one layer type per panel and stack panels in the layout instead. The `dataColorsEditorConfig` thresholds live in `context` on the viz - same pattern as the marker `dataColors` panel above.

viz_background_color.title: 10. backgroundColor
viz_background_color.description: Custom panel background colour visible behind the tile gaps. Subtle but useful for theming.

viz_choro_opacity_tooltip.title: 11. Choropleth - opacity 0.5 + tooltip header `country`
viz_choro_opacity_tooltip.description: Choropleth layer with `choroplethOpacity: 0.5` and `tooltipHeaderField` bound to the country column. `additionalTooltipFields` adds the metric below the header. Lower opacity makes the underlying basemap visible through the polygons - useful when geography matters as much as the metric.

viz_choro_result_limit.title: 12. Choropleth - resultLimit=1
viz_choro_result_limit.description: Per-layer `resultLimit: 1` caps the choropleth to a single shaded country (the first row from the SPL output). Useful for `top 1` country highlight or single-region drilldown panels.

viz_choro_geom_string.title: 13. Choropleth - geom data formatted as string
viz_choro_geom_string.description: Some pipelines emit the `geom` column as a serialized JSON string instead of an object. SPL pattern: `| geom geo_countries featureIdField=country | where isnotnull(geom) | eval geom=tostring(geom)`. The map runtime parses the string back to GeoJSON. **Gotcha 1 (NULL string)**: `tostring()` on a NULL `geom` row produces the literal string `"Null"`, which the viz then tries to JSON.parse - giving `Unexpected token 'N', "Null" is not valid JSON`. **Gotcha 2 (lookup keys)**: `geo_countries` matches on the full English country name (`United States`, `United Kingdom`, `Brazil`, ...), NOT on ISO-2 codes (`US`, `GB`, `BR`). ISO-2 keys silently produce NULL rows for every country - which then triggers Gotcha 1. Always filter `where isnotnull(geom)` *before* `tostring()`.

viz_choro_world_styled.title: 14. Choropleth - World, no chrome, black stroke, grey empty
viz_choro_world_styled.description: World choropleth driven by `source: "geo://default/world"` (built-in geo source - no `geom` command needed in the SPL). All chrome disabled: `showBaseLayer: false`, `showZoomControls: false`, `showScale: false`. `choroplethStrokeColor: #000000`, `choroplethEmptyAreaColor: #888888`, `choroplethOpacity: 1.0`. `zoom: 0.5` + explicit `center` for a full-world embed-style frame.

viz_choro_us_styled.title: 15. Choropleth - US, grey BG, RangeValue palette
viz_choro_us_styled.description: US-states choropleth driven by `source: "geo://default/us"`. Grey panel background, grey strokes, white empty states, `choroplethOpacity: 1.0`. `dataColors` uses `rangeValue` over the count column for explicit traffic-light bucketing instead of the default gradient. `zoom: 3` + center over the contiguous US.
